search_terms_used: sleep athletic performance endurance runners systematic review PubMed
sleep deprivation endurance performance running meta-analysis
sleep extension athletes performance recovery study
napping athletic performance endurance sport research
sleep quality injury risk athletes prospective study
recovery modalities running performance cold water immersion compression
sleep hygiene education intervention athletes randomized controlled trial PubMed
partial sleep deprivation endurance performance running self-paced PubMed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage across sleep deprivation (2 meta-analyses), sleep extension, napping, sleep hygiene, injury risk, and recovery modalities. Includes 4 systematic reviews/meta-analyses, 1 RCT, 1 prospective cohort, and 4 narrative/review papers. Populations span elite, collegiate, recreational, and endurance athletes. One running-specific RCT included. Cold water immersion meta-analysis covers recovery modalities. Gap: no single paper on sleep monitoring via wearables in athletes.

papers.title: The Impact of Sleep Interventions on Athletic Performance: A Systematic Review
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed 25 studies on sleep interventions (hygiene, naps, extension, light manipulation) and their effects on athletic performance.

papers.title: How much does sleep deprivation impair endurance performance? A systematic review and meta-analysis
papers.notes: Meta-analyzed 31 studies (38 effect sizes) quantifying the moderate negative effect of sleep deprivation on endurance performance, with greater impairment in exercises >30 min.

papers.title: Effects of Acute Sleep Loss on Physical Performance: A Systematic and Meta-Analytical Review
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed and meta-analyzed the effects of acute sleep loss on multiple physical performance domains including endurance, strength, and power.

papers.title: Sleep extension in athletes: what we know so far - A systematic review
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed evidence on sleep extension interventions (46-113 min beyond habitual ~7 h) and their effects on sport performance in athletes.

papers.title: The effects of sleep extension on the athletic performance of collegiate basketball players
papers.notes: Measured effects of extending sleep to ~10 hours per night on sprint times, shooting accuracy, reaction time, and mood in collegiate basketball players.

papers.title: To Nap or Not to Nap? A Systematic Review Evaluating Napping Behavior in Athletes and the Impact on Various Measures of Athletic Performance
papers.notes: Systematically reviewed napping prevalence, duration, timing, and effects on physical and cognitive performance in athletes, recommending 20-90 min naps between 13:00-16:00.

papers.title: Sleep and Athletic Performance: Impacts on Physical Performance, Mental Performance, Injury Risk and Recovery, and Mental Health
papers.notes: Comprehensively reviewed sleep's multidimensional impact on athletic performance, injury risk, recovery, and mental health across sport populations.

papers.title: General health complaints and sleep associated with new injury within an endurance sporting population: A prospective study
papers.journal: Journal of Science and Medicine in Sport
papers.notes: Prospectively tracked 95 endurance athletes over 52 weeks to examine associations between sleep quantity, subjective health complaints, and new injury occurrence.

papers.title: Sleep Hygiene for Optimizing Recovery in Athletes: Review and Recommendations
papers.notes: Reviewed sleep hygiene strategies and their evidence base for optimizing recovery in athletes, providing practical recommendations for sleep environment, habits, and routines.

papers.title: Partial sleep deprivation affects endurance performance and psychophysiological responses during 12-minute self-paced running exercise
papers.notes: Randomized crossover trial measuring the effect of partial sleep deprivation (beginning vs. end of night) on 12-minute self-paced running distance and psychophysiological responses.
